labels.text: M1 — 150 HP 600V 140A FLA Design B SF 1.15 TEFC
Pickup 140 A = 1.0× FLA (Class 10 thermal model, SF 1.15 allowed)
Pickup 1200 A primary = 1.33× LRA — clears short-circuits without nuisance-tripping on locked rotor
Pickup 175 A primary = 1.25× FLA × 1.15 SF, VI curve TMS 0.3 — locked-rotor / stall backup if 49 thermal misses
Hand reset — converges trips from 49, 50, 51, and the PTC chain (via 49 input)
Class F insulation hot-spot threshold ~155 °C — PTC R switches from <100 ohm cold to >2700 ohm hot at threshold

protection_settings.set_unit: A FLA × Class 10 thermal curve (pickup 1.0× FLA, Service Factor 1.15 allowed in the thermal model so the relay does NOT trip until current sustained above 1.15× FLA = 161 A for the Class 10 time-current envelope)
protection_settings.time_curve: Class 10 thermal envelope (NEC § 430.32(A)(1) — 115% of FLA for SF ≥ 1.15)

protection_settings.set_unit: A primary (1.33× locked-rotor 900 A — clears bolted short-circuit without nuisance-tripping on legitimate LRA inrush)
protection_settings.time_curve: instantaneous, no intentional delay (~25 ms total operate)

protection_settings.set_unit: A primary (1.25× FLA × 1.15 SF — backup against locked-rotor / stall if 49 thermal model fails)
protection_settings.time_curve: IEC VI (very inverse), TMS 0.3 — operates ~6 s at 900 A LRA, below the Design B locked-rotor withstand time of 12 s

protection_settings.set_unit: Hand-reset master trip — operator must press reset before Q1 can be re-closed; ensures no auto-reclose onto a faulted or hot motor

assumptions: Multifunction motor IED (SEL-710, Schneider Sepam M41, or ABB REM615) assumed for R49 / R50 / R51 — modern numerical relays bundle 49/50/51/86 + thermistor input + 46/47/48/49S into a single chassis. The schematic shows them as discrete items per protection-function for zone clarity, but the physical implementation is a single device.

ANSI 49 thermal element implemented via the relay's thermal-replica algorithm — there is no dedicated 'thermal_relay' value in the schematic IR device_class enum (the 27-value enum includes idmt_relay, instantaneous_relay, differential_relay, ref_relay, lockout_relay, uv_relay, ov_relay, distance_relay but no thermal_relay). The device_class field is therefore 'idmt_relay' (the closest match because the relay's thermal element operates on an inverse-time current curve) with the ANSI function code '49' carrying the actual protection function.

Class 10 thermal class selected per IEC 60947-4-1 Table 2 and aligned with NEMA MG-1 / IEEE 1068 Design B motor characteristics: 12 s locked-rotor withstand time → Class 10 (tolerates 10 s at 7.2× rated current) — comfortably inside the Design B withstand envelope.

Service Factor 1.15 incorporated into the 49 thermal-model pickup: the relay does not trip until current sustained above 1.15× FLA = 161 A for the Class 10 time-current curve. NEC 2023 § 430.32(A)(1) permits 115% of FLA for SF ≥ 1.15 motors (vs 125% for SF=1.0 motors — but 115% sits within 125% so the more-conservative 115% applies).
R50 instantaneous pickup at 1.33× LRA per IEEE C37.96 § 5.5 recommended factor (1.3× to 1.5× LRA depending on cold-start vs warm-start sensitivity).

fault-level intent consumed: ifault_ka_lv = 14 kA at the motor terminals. Q1 MCCB rated 35 kAIC — comfortably above the available fault current, so the MCCB will safely interrupt a bolted short-circuit per NEC § 110.10 interrupting-rating requirement. 35 kAIC is the standard catalogue rating for 200 AF MCCBs at 600 V from Eaton / Schneider / ABB / Siemens / GE.

db-layout-rollup intent consumed: motor MCC bucket fed_from = DB-MOTOR-MCC, main_switch_rating_a = 200 A — matches Q1 MCCB AF rating (200 AF), with the Q1 trip-unit setting at 1.25× FLA per NEC § 430.52(C)(1) Table 430.52 for inverse-time CB (max 250% for Design B); 1.25× = 175 A is therefore within the 250% × 140 = 350 A limit.

rationale.chat_summary: 150 HP 600 V Design B motor protection schematic (US jurisdiction, NEC framework). R49 thermal (Class 10, 140 A FLA, SF 1.15) + R50 instantaneous (1200 A = 1.33× LRA) + R51 IDMT VI backup + R86 hand-reset lockout, plus motor-embedded PTC thermistor chain B1 wired to R49 thermistor input. db-layout-rollup + fault-level intents both consumed. Reply to refine.

sections.title: Schematic Identification
sections.summary: protection_motor on sheet PROT-004 for a US industrial 150 HP, 600 V, 140 A FLA, Design B, Service Factor 1.15, Class F insulation TEFC motor. Two intents consumed: db-layout-rollup (motor MCC bucket fed_from = DB-MOTOR-MCC, main_switch_rating_a = 200 A) and fault-level (ifault_ka_lv = 14 kA at motor terminals). US jurisdiction applies NEC 2023 Article 430 (motor circuits) + § 430.32 (overload) + § 430.52 (short-circuit and ground-fault), IEEE C37.96 (motor protection guide), IEEE Std 315 (US symbol library, cross-referenced with BS EN 60617 on bs_en_60617_ref + ieee_std_315_ref item fields).

decisions.label: Multifunction IED with discrete schematic representation
decisions.summary: R49/R50/R51 are functions of a single motor IED (SEL-710 / Sepam M41 / REM615) drawn as discrete items for zone clarity.

decisions.label: Two consumed intents — full hybrid mode
decisions.summary: db-layout-rollup + fault-level intents both consumed; settings cross-checked against fault level 14 kA and MCC bucket rating 200 A.
decisions.rule: Hybrid-mode execution per generator prompt Step 11

sections.title: Source Context
sections.summary: 600 V three-phase, supplied from a motor control centre (MCC) bucket via a 200 AF MCCB Q1. Motor M1: 150 HP, 600 V, 140 A FLA, 900 A locked-rotor (LRA = 6.4× FLA, consistent with NEMA Design B Code G typical), Service Factor 1.15, Class F insulation (155 °C hot-spot threshold). fault-level intent supplied 14 kA at the motor terminals; db-layout-rollup intent supplied main_switch_rating_a = 200 A. Q1 MCCB rated 200 AF / 35 kAIC matches both intents.

decisions.label: CT primary 200 A at 1.43× FLA
decisions.summary: 200/5 CT primary covers up to ~280 A continuous (40% overload headroom) — comfortable margin above 140 A FLA without compressing the protection setpoint range.
decisions.rule: CT ratio selection for motor protection

decisions.label: fault-level intent consumed: 14 kA
decisions.summary: Available fault current at motor terminals comfortably below Q1 MCCB interrupting rating 35 kAIC.

decisions.label: db-layout-rollup intent consumed: 200 A MCC bucket
decisions.summary: main_switch_rating_a = 200 A matches Q1 AF rating; NEC § 430.52 short-circuit protection sizing satisfied.

sections.title: Items Breakdown
sections.summary: 12 devices placed: 3× CTs T1A/T1B/T1C (200/5 class 5P20, one per phase), R49 (thermal-replica relay, ANSI 49, device_class = idmt_relay because no dedicated thermal_relay enum value exists), R50 (instantaneous OC, ANSI 50), R51 (IDMT VI backup, ANSI 51), R86 (hand-reset lockout, ANSI 86), B1 (motor-embedded PTC thermistor chain, 6 thermistors, 2 per phase, wired through 3-wire connection to R49's thermistor input), Q1 (600 V 200 AF 35 kAIC MCCB with shunt trip coil), M1 (the 150 HP motor itself), plus three terminal blocks X1 (CT secondary shorting), X2 (PTC 3-wire), X3 (125 VDC trip circuit). Every item carries ieee_std_315_ref alongside bs_en_60617_ref because the US drafting convention dual-references both symbol standards on motor protection schematics.

decisions.label: device_class judgment call: R49 → idmt_relay (no thermal_relay enum)
decisions.summary: The 27-value schema enum has idmt_relay / instantaneous_relay / differential_relay / ref_relay / lockout_relay / uv_relay / ov_relay / distance_relay but no thermal_relay; the relay's thermal-replica algorithm operates on an inverse-time current curve so idmt_relay is the closest match. ANSI function code '49' carries the actual protection function.
decisions.rule: device_class enum mapping for thermal-replica protection

decisions.label: PTC chain B1 wired to R49 thermistor input
decisions.summary: Motor-embedded PTC thermistors (6 in total, 2 per phase, series chain) report winding hot-spot temperature to the relay; PTC resistance switches abruptly from <100 ohm cold to >2700 ohm at threshold (~155 °C for Class F insulation).
decisions.rule: PTC thermistor scheme for motor thermal protection
decisions.code_clause: IEC 60034-11 (rotating machines — thermal protection) + IEEE C37.96 § 5.6

decisions.label: Dual symbol-reference: BS EN 60617 + IEEE Std 315
decisions.summary: US drafting convention dual-references both standards on motor protection schematics — the bs_en_60617_ref field carries the v1.0 library symbol_id, the ieee_std_315_ref field carries the IEEE Std 315 figure number for the same symbol.
decisions.code_clause: IEEE Std 315-1975 (graphical symbols for electrical and electronic diagrams)

sections.title: Protection Coordination
sections.summary: Four protection layers in coordinated sequence. R49 (thermal): pickup 1.0× FLA (140 A) with Service Factor 1.15 allowed in the thermal model (no trip until sustained > 1.15× FLA = 161 A), Class 10 envelope — tolerates 7.2× FLA for ~10 s (LRA at start). R50 (instantaneous): pickup 1200 A primary (1.33× LRA per IEEE C37.96 § 5.5) — clears bolted short-circuits in ~25 ms without nuisance-tripping on legitimate LRA inrush. R51 (IDMT VI): pickup 175 A (1.25× FLA × 1.15 SF) on VI curve TMS 0.3 — operates ~6 s at 900 A LRA, below the Design B 12 s locked-rotor withstand time. R86 (lockout): hand-reset trip from any of R49/R50/R51 — Q1 cannot reclose until operator presses reset.

decisions.label: R49 Class 10 thermal envelope with SF 1.15 margin
decisions.summary: Class 10 tolerates 7.2× FLA for ~10 s — comfortably inside Design B 12 s locked-rotor withstand. SF 1.15 margin permitted per NEC § 430.32(A)(1) for SF ≥ 1.15 motors.
decisions.rule: Thermal class selection for Design B motor with SF

decisions.label: R50 pickup 1.33× LRA
decisions.summary: 1.33× LRA (1200 A) sits between IEEE C37.96 § 5.5 recommended factors of 1.3× and 1.5× — clears short-circuits without nuisance-tripping on legitimate LRA.
decisions.rule: Instantaneous OC pickup for motor protection

decisions.label: R51 backup at 1.25× FLA × 1.15 SF
decisions.summary: Backup IDMT against thermal model failure — pickup 175 A on VI curve TMS 0.3 trips at ~6 s on LRA, within Design B 12 s withstand.
decisions.rule: Backup IDMT settings for motor protection

sections.title: Compliance Assessment
sections.summary: Compliant against NEC 2023 Article 430 (motor circuits) — specifically § 430.32 (overload protection), § 430.52 (short-circuit and ground-fault protection), and § 430.52(C)(1) Table 430.52 (short-circuit device sizing); IEEE C37.96 (motor protection guide); NEC § 110.10 (interrupting rating verification); IEEE Std 315 (US symbol cross-reference); IEC 60034-11 (rotating machines thermal protection); BS EN 60617 IEC 617-7 (graphical symbols, cross-referenced alongside IEEE Std 315 per the US dual-reference convention). No CRITICAL or HIGH flags. Seven assumptions documented including the multifunction IED choice, ANSI 49 device_class judgment call, Class 10 thermal selection, SF 1.15 application, R50 pickup factor derivation, and both consumed-intent declarations.

decisions.label: US jurisdiction citation discipline
decisions.summary: NEC 2023 Article 430 cited directly as the operative US installation standard; IEEE C37.96 + IEEE Std 315 cross-cited as US protection / symbol references; IEC 60034-11 cited as the jurisdiction-agnostic motor-thermal standard.

decisions.label: NEC § 430.32(A)(1) for SF ≥ 1.15 thermal margin
decisions.summary: 115% of FLA permitted for SF ≥ 1.15 motors (vs 125% for SF = 1.0); the 115% sits within the 125% so the more-conservative 115% applies in the thermal model.
decisions.rule: NEC overload sizing per service factor

sections.title: Cross-Skill Cascade Verification
sections.summary: fault-level intent supplied ifault_ka_lv = 14 kA at motor terminals — comfortably below Q1 MCCB 35 kAIC interrupting rating per NEC § 110.10. db-layout-rollup intent supplied main_switch_rating_a = 200 A for the MCC bucket — matches Q1 AF rating. The Q1 trip-unit setting at 1.25× FLA = 175 A satisfies NEC § 430.52(C)(1) Table 430.52 short-circuit device limit (max 250% × 140 = 350 A for inverse-time CB on Design B motor). All cascade values cross-verified.

decisions.label: fault-level cross-check passed
decisions.summary: 14 kA available < 35 kAIC Q1 interrupting rating — NEC § 110.10 satisfied.

decisions.label: db-layout-rollup cross-check passed
decisions.summary: 200 A MCC bucket = Q1 200 AF — sensor / load-current alignment.

sections.title: Schedule Notes
sections.summary: Hybrid-mode execution explicit: db-layout-rollup + fault-level intents both consumed. earthing intent not present and not required for this motor protection scheme (motor earth-fault is covered by R51 residual element on the same phase CTs, not by a dedicated earthing-system reference). No tool_call_pending entries — all four protection_settings entries fully resolved at LLM-emission time using the consumed fault-level value (14 kA → Q1 35 kAIC verification) and the consumed db-layout-rollup value (200 A → Q1 200 AF matching). The schematic represents a single motor's protection circuit; the MCC bucket itself + its short-circuit coordination with the upstream feeder are covered on the sibling MCC schematic, not duplicated here.

decisions.label: Hybrid-mode 2-of-3 intent consumption
decisions.summary: db-layout-rollup + fault-level consumed; earthing not required for the motor zone (residual EF carried by phase CTs).

decisions.label: No tool_call_pending — settings hand-verifiable
decisions.summary: All four protection_settings entries fully resolved against consumed intent values; no deferred calc.
decisions.rule: tool_call_pending discipline per generator prompt Step 8
